articles.title: KnightBound beginner's guide - 6 tips for achieved medieval mastery
articles.description: If you've lost faith in the idea that there are still creative people out there, I urge you to take a look at KnightBound. The introduction aims to assure you that it is a passion project by a solo developer who has been working on it and continues to do so. Of course, it has a free version, and it includes additional bells and whistles if you're willing to share some currency to support an indie creator. The goal is to create a retro-style first-person RPG dungeon-crawler and open-world experience for modern devices such as tablets and smartphones. It's already got a lot going for it, so there's plenty of adventure for you to find. Best iOS games to play on your iPhone in 2026 ... [ MORE ]

articles.title: MAGA Melts Down Over Days-Old Jimmy Kimmel Trump ‘Widow’ Joke
articles.description: MANDEL NGAN / AFP via Getty Images President Donald Trump’s most ardent supporters are calling for Jimmy Kimmel to be fired over a days-old joke about Melania Trump. The comedian said the first lady had the glow of an “expectant widow” during his show on Thursday, when he parodied the White House Correspondents’ Dinner. Barely two days later, a gunman stormed the event, firing shots before being apprehended by police. Now, MAGA is angry with Kimmel, even though his joke was delivered before Saturday night’s drama at the Washington Hilton. MAGA darling Gunther Eagleman, whose X account is a goldmine of Trumpian rhetoric, declared , “Fire Jimmy Kimmel!” Read more at The Daily Beast.

articles.title: Why CTTO is not enough: The invisible theft in our daily scrolls
articles.description: CEBU CITY, Philippines — We’ve all seen it. You’re scrolling through your feed and come across a stunning photo of a sunset or a viral funny video, and the caption simply reads: “CTTO” or “Credits to the owner—no infringement intended.” In our heads, it feels like a polite digital disclaimer. It’s as if those four letters act as a magic spell that protects us from getting into trouble while we share something we love. But behind the convenience of that quick repost is a reality many of us aren’t ready to hear. READ: SiloyAsks: Netizens split on proposed social media [...]... Keep on reading: Why CTTO is not enough: The invisible theft in our daily scrolls
